bullets.text: Iran war drives largest-ever oil supply disruption; high prices and Strait of Hormuz risk amplify global inflation and recession fears.
Oil whipsaws on war-end signals and blockade threats; markets reprice stagflation risk as crude moves toward/away from $100.
Iran warns tanker traffic; analysts flag LNG as potentially more vulnerable than oil if Hormuz closes, impacting power and industrial costs.
G7 energy ministers consider coordinated strategic reserve releases to stabilize prices amid Middle East conflict-driven supply shocks.
Asian governments and markets scramble to limit fallout from higher energy prices; regional equities rebound when oil drops on ceasefire chatter.
France deploys a major naval force to the Middle East, signaling escalation management and potential protection of sea lanes.
U.S. rates react to war news: Treasury yields pause/rally on expectations of de-escalation, highlighting geopolitics’ dominance over macro data.
Federal Reserve posts 2025 Q4 Financial Accounts preview and approves a CBS Banc-Corp notice, signaling routine supervisory and data updates.
Pentagon blacklists Anthropic over supply-chain risk; Anthropic sues, raising procurement uncertainty for frontier AI vendors and defense AI strategy.
OpenAI buys Promptfoo to harden AI agents; Microsoft adds higher-priced Office tier with Copilot, accelerating monetization and security focus in enterprise AI.
Foreclosures reportedly rise as household stress builds; underscores uneven “K-shaped” recovery pressures on lower-income groups and women’s spending power.
Climate science signals: studies report possible acceleration in global warming; policy and markets face higher tail risks for extreme-weather costs.
